content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Fixed the too-coarse durably_verified gate in verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success (success_reconciliation.rs). The previous implementation keyed the HashSet only by mapped_to_bead_id, so if two distinct findings both mapped to the same existing bead and persisting the verified record succeeded for the first but failed for the second, the shared bead id in the set would incorrectly allow Phase 3 to post a comment for the unpersisted second mapping. On replay, the second mapping would still be unverified, causing a duplicate comment. Changed from HashSet<&str> keyed by mapped_to_bead_id to HashSet<usize> keyed by outcome index, giving each mapping independent persist-success tracking. Phase 2 now enumerates outcomes and inserts the index on persist success. Phase 3 enumerates outcomes and checks the index set, ensuring a 1:1 correspondence between durable persistence and comment posting regardless of how many findings share the same mapped_to_bead_id.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Changed durably_verified from HashSet<&str> (keyed by mapped_to_bead_id) to durably_verified_indices as HashSet<usize> (keyed by outcome index) in verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success. Phase 2 now uses enumerate() and inserts the index on persist success. Phase 3 uses enumerate() and checks durably_verified_indices.contains(&idx) instead of the old mapped_to_bead_id lookup, ensuring per-mapping independence.
2. [Completed] Ran cargo test (758 passed, 0 failed), cargo clippy (no new warnings from success_reconciliation.rs), cargo fmt --check (clean).

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 758 passed, 0 failed across all test suites (683 lib, 174 integration, 1 unit, 9 doc-tests ignored)
- cargo fmt --check: exit 0, no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: no new warnings from success_reconciliation.rs; all warnings are pre-existing in unrelated files
- Amendment verified: durably_verified_indices is HashSet<usize> created at line 771, Phase 2 inserts idx at line 794 on persist success, Phase 3 checks durably_verified_indices.contains(&idx) at line 806. Two findings mapped to the same bead are now tracked independently by their position in the outcomes vector.
- Files modified: src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs (durably_verified_indices refactor)

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing clippy warnings (19 from code outside this change) cause cargo clippy -- -D warnings to fail; none are introduced by this change.
- The br_show_error_is_missing pattern for stale-bead detection is duplicated between cli/run.rs and planned_elsewhere.rs; a future consolidation could extract this into a shared utility on BrError.
- With the split verification/commenting approach, if a crash occurs after persisting verified=true but before posting the comment, the comment is permanently lost. This is an accepted tradeoff since comments are best-effort and the alternative (duplicate comments on replay) is worse.
